properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: If this value is on, makefiles will be generated without the .SILENT directive, and all commands will be echoed to the console during the make.  This is useful for debugging only. With Visual Studio IDE projects all commands are done without /nologo.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Disable NOCMOV.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Disable SSE2.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Permanently disable the FFI extension to reduce the size of the LuaJIT executable. But please consider that the FFI library is compiled-in, but NOT loaded by default. It only allocates any memory, if you actually make use of it.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Disable the JIT compiler, i.e. turn LuaJIT into a pure interpreter.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Features from Lua 5.2 that are unlikely to break existing code are enabled by default. Some other features that *might* break some existing code (e.g. __pairs or os.execute() return values) can be enabled here. Note: this does not provide full compatibility with Lua 5.2 at this time.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: This is the client for the GDB JIT API. GDB 7.0 or higher is required to make use of it. See lj_gdbjit.c for details. Enabling this causes a non-negligible overhead, even when not running under GDB.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Use the system provided memory allocator (realloc) instead of the bundled memory allocator. This is slower, but sometimes helpful for debugging. It's helpful for Valgrind's memcheck tool, too. This option cannot be enabled on x64, since the built-in allocator is mandatory.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: This option is required to run LuaJIT under Valgrind. The Valgrind header files must be installed. You should enable debug information, too.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Use only ansi features.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Build wluajit interpreter for no-console applications.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Environment variable to use as package.cpath.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: gives the maximum size for the description of the source.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Environment variable for initial script.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Max. pattern captures.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Is the maximum length for an input line in the stand-alone interpreter.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Environment variable to use as package.path.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Is the default prompt used by stand-alone Lua.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Is the default continuation prompt used by stand-alone Lua.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Turn on assertions for the Lua/C API to debug problems with lua_* calls. This is rather slow, use only while developing C libraries/embeddings.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Turn on assertions for the whole LuaJIT VM. This significantly slows down everything. Use only if you suspect a problem with LuaJIT itself.

properties.name: HELPSTRING
properties.value: Use modified loadlib.c with support for relative paths on posix systems.
